---
title: "Research Questions"
format: html
bibliography: ../references.bib
---

# Research Questions

This page keeps the longer-lived research questions that sit underneath the
current implementation work. For the actively changing forward-looking
scratchpad, use [Ideas and Future Directions](ideas.qmd).

## Oracle supervision

- Which oracle RRI formulation best predicts meaningful reconstruction improvement in ASE?
- How should candidate-view point clouds be sampled so they remain comparable to the semi-dense SLAM state?
- Where does Chamfer distance remain good enough, and where do we need better point-to-surface metrics?

## RRI prediction models

- Which candidate-conditioning strategy works best: explicit projection features, learned positional encodings, or a hybrid?
- Which EVL-derived signals are truly necessary for high-quality RRI ranking?
- How should the scorer represent uncertainty, coverage, and out-of-bounds candidates without collapsing back to weak coverage proxies?

## EFMs as the NBV backbone

- Can EVL remain an effective frozen encoder for NBV, or do we eventually need partial fine-tuning or a different backbone?
- How can we update scene state incrementally as new observations arrive without recomputing the full stack?
- Which previously computed features or intermediate states can be reused cheaply across sequential decisions?

## Entity-aware objectives

- Can RRI be decomposed into scene-level and entity-level terms in a way that is useful for view selection?
- Which entity signals are most helpful: GT/predicted OBBs, semantic segmentation, or object-centric visibility cues?
- How should user intent or task weights enter an entity-aware NBV objective?

## Continuous and multi-step planning

- When should the project stay with discrete candidate ranking, and when is it worth moving to continuous pose prediction?
- How can we formulate multi-step RL when counterfactual views do not have the full modality set of the recorded ego-trajectory?
- Which state, reward, and transition abstractions make actor-critic or planner-style methods realistic for this setting?

## Human-facing systems

- How can the system explain why a view is recommended?
- What role, if any, should language or multimodal assistants play in user interaction, high-level planning, or entity selection?
